---
title: "Career"
---

::: {.hero-banner}

Climate Scientist and Digital Science Communicator with over ten years of experience working with climate data, increasingly drawn to the space where science meets storytelling.

My career has evolved from atmospheric modelling and climate analysis to building visual products, interactive dashboards and communication materials. That intersection, between rigorous science and engaging communication, is where I want to keep growing.

Currently, I create visual products and contribute to climate services for the Spanish Meteorological Service, while completing a postgraduate programme in Science Communication to consolidate this transition.
:::

::: {.women-box}
## Academic Background

#### 2027 - Postgraduate in Science Communication  
**International University of Valencia**  
*In progress · Part-time online while working full time*

#### 2020 — PhD in Atmospheric Physics  
**University of Santiago de Compostela**  
*Grade: Merit · European Doctorate Mention*  

Thesis: *Precipitation Recycling and the Importance of the Land-Atmosphere Interactions over the European Continent*  

[Read the PhD dissertation](http://hdl.handle.net/10347/23330)

#### 2014 — MSc in Meteorology  
**Universitat de Barcelona**  

Relevant subjects: Dynamic Meteorology · Physics of the Atmosphere · Climate and Climate Change · Atmospheric Pollution · Hydrometeorology · Meteorological Modelling · Forecasting  

Master’s thesis: *Effect of lateral boundary conditions in the WRF limited-area model*

#### 2013 — BSc in Physics  
**University of Santiago de Compostela**  

Degree thesis (*with honours*): *Precipitation recycling in Europe — moisture tracers method for computation of recycling rate*
:::

## Career Path

::: {.women-box}
### 2024–Present · Climate Scientist  
**Tragsatec – AEMET**

Working at the interface between climate science and user-oriented services, translating seasonal prediction outputs into accessible information for decision-making contexts such as agriculture and water management.

**Key contributions**
- Developed interactive web dashboards and graphics to communicate seasonal weather prediction to non-specialist users
- Translated climate data into visual narratives through maps, plots and digital interfaces
- Produced written briefings and communication materials for institutional stakeholders such as AEMET, ECMWF and DWD

[Have a look at the project results](https://dashboard-tragsatec.onrender.com/){.home-link}

**Tools:** D3.js · Python · Tableau · Flourish · Illustrator · JupyterHub
:::

::: {.women-box}
### 2023–2024 — WRF Specialist  
**MeteoGRID S.L.**

Designed and executed high-resolution WRF-SFIRE coupled atmosphere–fire simulations for wildfire risk assessment and post-fire analysis across the Iberian Peninsula. Configured model physics, nesting strategies and fuel datasets for each case, and post-processed outputs to generate operational fire-spread maps, wind profiles and spotting-risk products.

**Tools:** Python · R · WRF-SFIRE · HPC · Bash · NCO · GrADS
:::

::: {.women-box}
### 2018–2021 — Climate Scientist  
**Meteogalicia**

Participated in a multi-institutional oceanographic research project, coordinating data and deliverables among partner institutions. Processed and quality-controlled large oceanographic datasets and conducted statistical analyses to characterise interannual variability and trends along the Galician coast.

Also authored technical reports and scientific deliverables for national and EU-funded milestones.

**Tools:** Python · HPC · Bash · LaTeX · QGIS
:::

::: {.women-box}
### 2015–2016 — Broadcast Meteorologist  
**Santiago TV**

Delivered daily on-air weather reports for a general audience, translating technical meteorological information into clear, engaging communication. Also designed broadcast graphics and visual materials to support forecast storytelling in a live news environment.

**Tools:** Broadcast graphics · Science communication · Live presenting
:::

::: {.women-box}
### 2018 — Paleoclimate Researcher  
**University College London**

- Developed a Python numerical model to simulate speleothem growth as a function of drip rate, cave temperature and pCO₂, enabling forward modelling of proxy climate records
- Designed and populated a MySQL relational database for multi-proxy palaeoclimate datasets from the North Atlantic region

**Tools:** Python · MySQL · NumPy · Matplotlib
:::

::: {.women-box}
### 2014–2017 — PhD Candidate  
**University of Santiago de Compostela**

- Designed and carried out WRF mesoscale simulations to study land–surface–atmosphere feedbacks over Iberia and Europe
- Developed post-processing scripts in Python and GrADS for automated extraction, analysis and publication-ready figures
- Presented results at international conferences
- Mentored undergraduate students in programming and atmospheric modelling tasks
:::

::: {.women-box}
### Visiting PhD Candidate  
**University of Lisbon**

Six-month research visit to the Instituto Dom Luiz (IDL), collaborating on WRF–LEAF-Hydro land-surface model experiments.

**Tools:** Python · WRF · LEAF-Hydro · HPC · GrADS
:::

::: {.grid}
::: {.g-col-6}
::: {.women-box}
## Languages

- **Spanish:** Native
- **Galician:** Native
- **English:** C1 Advanced (certified in 2025)
- **Portuguese:** Intermediate
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-6}
::: {.women-box}
## Skills & Tech Tools

**Communication & Design:** PowerPoint · Canva · Illustrator · Adobe InDesign  

**Scientific & Data:** Python (Plotly, Shiny, Dash) · D3.js · QGIS · Flourish · SQL  

**Technical:** Bash/Linux · WRF model · HPC workflows · LaTeX
:::
:::
:::

::: {.women-box}
## Selected Training

### 2025 — Communication Course for Scientific Professionals  
Specialised training in science communication for technical and research profiles. *14 hours*

### 2022 — Introductory Course on GIS and Mapping with R  
Foundations of geospatial analysis and cartographic workflows in R. *20 hours*

### 2021 — Microsoft PowerPoint and Presentation Software – Intermediate Level  
Training in presentation design, multimedia content, animations and effective communication using PowerPoint. *25 hours*

### 2018 — ArcGIS Enterprise Workflows  
Training in enterprise GIS environments and workflow management. *20 hours*
:::

::: {.women-box}
## Publications & Contributions

### 2025 — *Hidden Women in Meteorology: Uncovering the Forgotten Pioneers and Their Legacy*  
**Article · Revista Tiempo y Clima**  
Science communication article translating the history of women in meteorology for a broad, non-specialist audience.  
**Sabela Sanfiz**

### 2025 — *Hidden Women in Meteorology: Uncovering the Forgotten Pioneers and Their Legacy*  
**Conference Poster · Spanish Climate Association International Congress**  
**Sabela Sanfiz**

### 2024 — *HiDALGO2 D3.1 Scalability, Optimization and Co-Design Activities*  
**Experiment findings**  
K. Nikas · P. Anastasiadis · A. Rivera Campos · M. Lawenda · et al.

### 2020 — *Precipitation Recycling and the Importance of the Land-Atmosphere Interactions over the European Continent*  
**PhD Thesis · University of Santiago de Compostela**  
**Sabela Sanfiz** · G. Miguez-Macho  
[hdl.handle.net/10347/23330](http://hdl.handle.net/10347/23330)

### 2020 — *The Utility of Land-Surface Model Simulations to Provide Drought Information in a Water Management Context*  
**Article · Water Resources Management**  
P. Quintana Seguí · A. Barella-Ortiz · **Sabela Sanfiz** · G. Miguez-Macho

### 2019 — *An Integrated Perspective of the Operational Forecasting System in Rías Baixas (Galicia, Spain)*  
**Book Chapter · Lecture Notes in Computer Science**  
A. Venâncio · P. Montero · P. Melo Costa · J. J. Taboada · et al.
:::
